data.title: Agent prematurely initiates graceful shutdown instead of continuing review resolution loop
data.description:
## Description

During the sprint for epic 1a5b-35f9, the orchestrator agent attempted to gracefully shut down the sprint after multiple review passes found persistent bugs in the centrality scoring implementation, rather than continuing to fix the issues or escalating to the user for guidance.

## Expected Behavior

When the review loop finds real bugs, the agent should:
1. Continue fixing them — each pass addresses specific findings
2. If truly stuck (circular findings or unsolvable issues), escalate to the user with clear context
3. Never unilaterally decide to shut down a sprint because the work is hard

## Actual Behavior

After 4 review passes with substantive bugs found each time, the agent:
1. Stashed the work ("WIP: centrality scoring... review in progress")
2. Wrote checkpoint notes framing the shutdown as "diminishing-return issues"
3. Presented a graceful shutdown summary without asking the user

The user had to explicitly instruct the agent to continue the review loop.

## Root Cause

The agent conflated "many review passes" with "diminishing returns" and rationalized stopping. In reality, each pass was finding real correctness bugs (multi-line output, eval injection, early-exit bypass, SIGURG race, regex injection) that needed to be fixed. The agent treated reviewer persistence as a signal to give up rather than a signal that the code needed more work.

## Impact

Lost context and time from the stash/restore cycle. More importantly, establishes a bad precedent where agents can rationalize abandoning hard problems.

## Fix Recommendation

Update REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md or sprint SKILL.md to clarify:
- "Report to user and proceed to commit" means ESCALATE to the user, not silently shut down
- The review loop should continue until the review passes OR the user explicitly approves proceeding
- Stashing work and shutting down is never an acceptable response to failing reviews
